rows.snippet: OmniPage OCR Cannot Process E-sized Images. 19 years ago OmniPage OCR Cannot Process E-sized Images. June 25, 2007 | KB: 1011839 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011839/omnipage-ocr-cannot-process-e-sized-images- Laserfiche 7.2.1, Import ...... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011839/omnipage-ocr-cannot-proces ...
rows.title: OmniPage OCR Cannot Process E-sized Images.
rows.detailTitle: OmniPage OCR Cannot Process E-sized Images.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith OmniPage OCR Cannot Process E-sized Images. Product: Laserfiche 7.2.1, Import Agent 7.0.2, Quick Fields 7.1.2, Scanning 7.2.1, Snapshot 7.0.3, Web Access 7.2.1 KB: 1011839 Published: June 25, 2007 Last Updated: June 25, 2007 Summary The OmniPage OCR Engine cannot process e-sized images. Resolution There is a hotfix available for Laserfiche products that include the OmniPage OCR engine. The fix includes updated versions of the following files: BPOmniOCR.exe (version 7.2.1.3) OmniOCRWrapper.dll (version 7.2.1.3) OmniPage32.lfo (version 1.0.0.3) To update the OmniPage OCR component Close any applications that may be using the OmniPage OCR engine. Click the following link to download a zip file containing the hotfix files. Hotfix_SCR27647.zip Replace your existing versions of BPOmniOCR.exe and OmniOCRWrapper.dll with the updated versions contained in the zip file. The files are located at "C:\Program Files\Common Files\Laserfiche\Batch Processor\BPOmniOCR." Register the new version of OmniOCRWrapper.dll. Click Start and then click Run. Type the following and then click OK: regsvr32 "C:\Program Files\Common Files\Laserfiche\Batch Processor\BPOmniOCR\OmniOCRWrapper.dll" Replace your existing version of OmniPage32.lfo located in your Laserfiche Client installation folder with the updated version. By default, the Laserfiche Client is installed at "C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Client." Laserfiche works around this size limitation in the OCR engine by processing an e-sized image in sections. Laserfiche generates OCR text for each section and then merges the results together to form the final OCR text pages. Be aware that this can occasionally result in words being split in the middle. However, text search functionality will not be affected as the search engine will merge the split words together during searches. Related Links BPOmniOCR.exe (version 7.2.1.3) also contains additional hotfixes not related to e-size processing. Please see the following Knowledge Base articles for more information. 1011725 OmniPage OCR Does Not Correctly Use the Image Orientation Displayed in the Laserfiche Client When OCRing. 1011840 Temporary OCR Files Are Not Automatically Removed. 1011841 OmniPage OCR Does Not Correctly Process Multi-Byte Western Languages. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 8.2.0 Hotfix 1012828 (August 2011). 14 years ago support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012828/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-weblink-8-2-0-hotfix-1012828-august-2011- WebLink 8.2.0 Summary Note: This hotfix is superseded by Knowledge ... no longe... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012828/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 8.2.0 Hotfix 1012828 (August 2011).
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 8.2.0 Hotfix 1012828 (August 2011).
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 8.2.0 Hotfix 1012828 (August 2011). Product: WebLink 8.2.0 KB: 1012828 Published: August 29, 2011 Last Updated: February 21, 2012 Summary Note: This hotfix is superseded by Knowledge Base article 1012885. This article document hotfix 1012828 for Laserfiche WebLink 8.2. This update includes the following changes: Opening embedded WebLink URLs from Microsoft Office applications no longer fail with the message "Unable to open [url]. Cannot download the information you requested." (84764) The WebLink Document Viewer now properly displays the values of multiple-value fields that are of types Text, Integer, Long Integer, and List. (77416) The Print (as PDF) option no longer displays a "The file is damaged and could not be repaired" error message for large documents when Adobe Acrobat Reader 9 or earlier is installed. (77338) WebLink now properly handles custom search forms that contain an Input element that binds to a Field search where the Input element is set to a List (single-select) or List (multi-select) input type and the list contains an item that is a blank space. Previously, such a search form would always append a search for a field value that is a blank space to the submitted search query. (75375) The update includes the following files: WebLink8.dll (8.2.0.260) WebLinkCommon.dll (8.2.0.260) WebLinkControls.dll (8.2.0.260) Resolution To update Laserfiche WebLink 8.2 Click on the following link to download a zip file containing hotfix 1012828. KB1012828.zip Extract the contents of the zip file to a temporary location and double-click the .msp file to update WebLink. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Constructing a WebLink URL that Links to a Document or Folder. 18 years ago KB: 1001086 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1001086/constructing-a-weblink-url-that-links-to-a-document-or-folder- WebLink 7 Summary You can specify a WebLink URL that links ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1001086/constructing-a-weblink-url ...
rows.title: Constructing a WebLink URL that Links to a Document or Folder.
rows.detailTitle: Constructing a WebLink URL that Links to a Document or Folder.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Constructing a WebLink URL that Links to a Document or Folder. Product: WebLink 7 KB: 1001086 Published: November 11, 2005 Last Updated: October 8, 2007 Summary You can specify a WebLink URL that links to a document or folder. More Information You can construct a URL that directly links to WebLink to perform the following: Directly open a specific document Directly open a specific folder Directly download an electronic document Directly download a specific document as a PDF Performing a search To directly open a specific document http://hostname/weblink7/docview.aspx?dbid=db&id=y To directly open a document to a specific page http://hostname/weblink7/DocView.aspx?dbid=db&id=y&page=z To directly open a specific folder http://hostname/weblink7/Browse.aspx?dbid=db&startid=y To directly download an electronic document http://hostname/weblink7/ElectronicFile.aspx?dbid=db&docid=y http://hostname/weblink7/docview.aspx?dbid=db&id=y&openfile="true" To directly download a specific document as a PDF http://hostname/weblink7/docview.aspx?dbid=db&id=y&openpdf="true" Note: The openpdf parameter will be ignored if your repository is configured to require an export reason. To perform a search http://hostname/weblink7/search.aspx?dbid=db&searchcommand=SearchSyntax Hostname is the name of the web server hosting WebLink. The value db specifies the dbid value of the WebLink repository configuration you want to use. The value y specifies the ID of the desired document or folder. The value SearchSyntax represents the search syntax of your desired search. Note: You can view the dbid for a repository configuration by loading the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility. Note: You can view the ID number for a document or folder by configuring the WebLink contents pane to display the ID column. Note: If WebLink has not been configured to use a repository configuration, then log in information is not provided in the URL. As a result, the Login page will prompt the user for this information. The desired document will be displayed after the user logs in to the repository. If a user is currently logged in to WebLink, then the user will not be prompted for log in information if you use the same Internet Explorer instance. However, if you use a different Internet Explorer instance, then the user will need to log in to the repository. Note: You cannot include special characters (e.g. #) in the search command without encoding the special characters. If your advanced search syntax contains special characters, encode the special characters by replacing the character with the percent sign (%) followed by the hexadecimal value associated with that special character. For example, the pound sign (#) has an associated hexadecimal value of 23. The following example will find all items with the "Customer" template where the "Phone#" field is "5551234." Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

Error Occurs When Loading the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility. 19 years ago default-web-site-quot-error-occurs-when-loading-the-laserfiche-weblink-7-administrator-39-s-utility- WebLink 7 Summary When you load the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's U... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011637/a-the-directory-weblink7-could-no ...
rows.title: A "The directory 'Weblink7' could not be found in the default web site." Error Occurs When Loading the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility.
rows.detailTitle: A "The directory 'Weblink7' could not be found in the default web site." Error Occurs When Loading the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "The directory 'Weblink7' could not be found in the default web site." Error Occurs When Loading the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility. Product: WebLink 7 KB: 1011637 Published: September 25, 2006 Last Updated: September 25, 2006 Summary When you load the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility, you may receive the following error message: The directory ''Weblink7'' could not be found in the default web site. You may have to use a different URL to access WebLink or manually configure IIS". More Information The Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility automatically checks that the "WebLink7" virtual directory exists under the "Default Web Site." While you can remove this default virtual directory created by the installation and use a different IIS website and virtual directory for your specific WebLink 7 installation, this check for the default virtual directory is built into the administrator's utility and cannot be disabled. This error message will only appear when loading the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility and does not impact users browsing your WebLink site. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Optimizing OCR for Speed Using the Registry 19 years ago registry Laserfiche 7.2.1, Import Agent 7.0.2, Snapshot 7.0.3, Integrator's Toolkit 7.2.1 Summary The OmniPage OCR engine allows you to choose between ... registy to specify which mode Impo... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011679/optimizing-ocr-for-speed-using-th ...
rows.title: Optimizing OCR for Speed Using the Registry
rows.detailTitle: Optimizing OCR for Speed Using the Registry
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Optimizing OCR for Speed Using the Registry Product: Laserfiche 7.2.1, Import Agent 7.0.2, Snapshot 7.0.3, Integrator's Toolkit 7.2.1 KB: 1011679 Published: October 31, 2006 Last Updated: November 1, 2006 Summary The OmniPage OCR engine allows you to choose between optimizing your OCR process for speed or accuracy. You can modify the registy to specify which mode Import Agent, Send to Laserfiche, Snapshot and Toolkit will use for OCR. By default, they will optimize the OCR process for accuracy. Note: The Laserfiche Client, Scanning, Web Access Scanning, and Quick Fields allow you to choose your optimization mode within the product itself. The registry modifications will not affect these products. However, OCR optimization cannot be configured for Send to Laserfiche in the Client. To configure OCR optimization for documents added to the repository using the Send to Laserfiche command from Microsoft Windows, please see "To optimize OCR in Import Agent, Send to Laserfiche, Snapshot and Toolkit for speed," below. Resolution By default, Import Agent, Send to Laserfiche, Snapshot and Toolkit will optimize their OCR processes for accuracy. If you would like to optimize these OCR processes for speed, follow the instructions below. To optimize OCR in Import Agent, Send to Laserfiche, Snapshot and Toolkit for speed Click the following link to download a zip file containing the relevant registry keys: OptimizeOmniPageOCR.zip Extract the contents of the zip file to a temporary location. Double-click the "Optimize OmniPage OCR for Speed.reg" file. You will be prompted to confirm that you wish to modify the registry. Select Yes. Note: To return to optimizing for accuracy, repeat this process with the "Optimize OmniPage OCR for Accuracy.reg" file. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Windows Vista Editions Without IIS Windows Authentication Cannot Host Laserfiche Web Products Using this Feature. 15 years ago Windows Vista Editions Without IIS Windows Authentication Cannot Host Laserfiche Web Products Using this Feature. September 27, 2010 | KB: 1012600 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012600/wind... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012600/windows-vista-editions-without-ii ...
rows.title: Windows Vista Editions Without IIS Windows Authentication Cannot Host Laserfiche Web Products Using this Feature.
rows.detailTitle: Windows Vista Editions Without IIS Windows Authentication Cannot Host Laserfiche Web Products Using this Feature.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Windows Vista Editions Without IIS Windows Authentication Cannot Host Laserfiche Web Products Using this Feature. Product: Web Access, WebLink, Audit Trail KB: 1012600 Published: September 27, 2010 Last Updated: September 27, 2010 Summary The following Windows Vista editions do not support Windows Authentication as an Internet Information Systems (IIS) feature: Basic, Starter, Premium. If you attempt to host a Laserfiche web product on this operating system, and this product attempts to use this IIS feature, authentication will fail and you will receive an error (in many cases, this error will either be a 500.19 or a 401 error). Note: You will receive the same error if the operating system supports Windows Authentication but this feature has not been enabled in IIS. The following list explains how this issue affects each Laserfiche web product: Web Access can optionally be configured to authenticate users via Windows Authentication. More info. WebLink can optionally be configured to authenticate users via Windows Authentication. More info. Audit Trail requires Windows Authentication, and cannot be hosted on one of the aforementioned Vista editions. More info. Agenda Manager does not use Windows Authentication. More Information IIS Features and Vista Editions Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

Error Occurs During the Activation Process. 16 years ago There was no endpoint listening at https://activation.laserfiche.com/IActivationService.svc that could accept the message." rows.title: A "There was no endpoint listening at https://activation.laserfiche.com/IActivationService.svc that could accept the message." Error Occurs During the Activation Process.
rows.detailTitle: A "There was no endpoint listening at https://activation.laserfiche.com/IActivationService.svc that could accept the message." Error Occurs During the Activation Process.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "There was no endpoint listening at https://activation.laserfiche.com/IActivationService.svc that could accept the message." Error Occurs During the Activation Process. Product: Quick Fields 8, Laserfiche Rio 8, Laserfiche Server - Avante 8 KB: 1012333 Published: September 4, 2009 Last Updated: September 4, 2009 Summary When going through the product activation process, you may receive the following error message: "There was no endpoint listening at https://activation.laserfiche.com/IActivationService.svc that could accept the message. This is often caused by an incorrect address or SOAP action. See InnerException, if present, for more details. Cause This issue can occur in certain situations when using the Windows default proxy settings for HTTPS. Workaround Manually edit a configuration file to ignore the default Windows proxy settings and to directly access the activation service without going through a proxy server. For Quick Fields 8, edit the Laserfiche.QuickFields.exe.config file. For Laserfiche Server 8, edit the ActivationTool.exe.config file. Using a text editor, do one of the following: Open the Laserfiche.QuickFields.exe.config file located in your Quick Fields installation folder. By default, Quick Fields is installed at "C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Quick Fields 8.0." Open the ActivationTool.exe.config file located in your Laserfiche Server 8 installation folder. By default, Laserfiche Server 8 is installed at "C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Server." In the <wsHttpBinding> section, locate the <binding> element's useDefaultWebProxy attribute and set it to false. Save your changes to the configuration file. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: New Features for Laserfiche 8.0.0. 15 years ago 8, Audit Trail 8.0.0, Scanning 8.0.0, Snapshot 8.0.0 Summary This article covers the following: New Features for Version 8.0.0 Key Differences from ... WMI) interface. Server Laserfiche Server ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011952/new-features-for-laserfiche-8-0-0 ...
rows.title: New Features for Laserfiche 8.0.0.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith New Features for Laserfiche 8.0.0. Product: Laserfiche 8, Audit Trail 8.0.0, Scanning 8.0.0, Snapshot 8.0.0 KB: 1011952 Published: March 31, 2008 Last Updated: February 22, 2011 Summary This article covers the following: New Features for Version 8.0.0 Key Differences from Laserfiche 7. More Information The following list highlights some of the major new features and changes in Laserfiche 8.0.0. New Features for version 8.0.0 Product New Feature Audit Trail Audit reporting now features a Web-based interface. Audit Trail Audit data is now stored in binary log files, instead of the main Laserfiche database. Audit Trail The reporting interface expands the options for filtering and refining audit reports. Client The Laserfiche Client now supports logging in to multiple Laserfiche repositories in the same Client window. Client Use the Document Viewer to add new types of annotations on an image (e.g. rectangles, lines, freehand drawing). Client The redesigned Options dialog box provides a cleaner method for browsing through available options without the need to open and close multiple dialog boxes. Client By default, there is now a navigation toolbar. Use the navigation toolbar to specify a specific path and quickly browse to the specified folder. Client The File menu now lists recently opened documents. Client The Client now utilizes a customizable toolbar system modeled after the Microsoft Office 2003 toolbar customization system. Client There is now a Quick Search toolbar to quickly perform text-based searches. Scanning Default settings in Laserfiche Scanning can now be different than default settings in the Laserfiche Client. For example, you may wish to use a different default naming convention when scanning versus importing through the Client. You can configure Laserfiche Scanning to use a different default document name, default template, and default volume. Scanning There is now a thumbnail view for scanned documents. Scanning User preferences are now stored as user attributes on the Laserfiche Server. Personal settings do not have to be reconfigured when moving to a different scan station. Server HTTP is now the basis for client/server communication in Laserfiche 8.0 using LFSO 8. Laserfiche Server 8.0 still supports legacy communications protocols (DCOM when using LFSO 7 and Sockets when using LFAPI) for backwards compatibility with previous client applications. By default, Laserfiche Server listens on port 80 (5050 when installed on the 32-bit edition of Windows XP). Server Laserfiche 8 now supports a Recycle Bin feature. If the feature is enabled, deleted entries are automatically placed in the Recycle Bin. Non-administrator users can view the Recycle Bin to see only those entries that they deleted. Administrators will be able to see all deleted items in the Recycle Bin. The Recycle Bin can be configured to periodically empty out items that have exceeded a certain time limit. Server You can now assign Laserfiche security items directly on Windows domain accounts without the need to directly link a Windows domain account with a Laserfiche user/group account. Server Laserfiche now stores a history that tracks changes to sticky note text. Server Annotations can now be marked as being protected. A protected annotation can only be modified or deleted by the creator of the annotation. Server Laserfiche 8 now supports new annotation types: File Attachment Image Callout Text Line Rectangle Strikeout Text Box Underline Freehand drawing Server You can now create fields separate from templates. One field can be associated with multiple templates. You can also assign fields to documents and folders without assigning a template to the document or folder. A field can now have multiple values assigned to it. In addition, Laserfiche 8 now includes the following new field types: time, number, and binary blob. Server You can now set security directly on a template. Server There are now entry access rights that control page deletion, page creation, and page movement. Server Laserfiche can now maintain an activity log that records entry-specific operations. The activity log is separate from auditing. Server Laserfiche Server 8 can send out notifications when changes are made to a document or folder. Server The search engine now runs as a separate service from the Laserfiche Server. The new search engine is Unicode compliant and supports multi-lingual search. Server You can now directly create copies of folders. Server Laserfiche 8 does not create the following log files: LFS.log, lfstartup.txt, error.log. Errors and information messages are now logged in the Windows event log (viewable from the Windows Event Viewer). The tracing system now uses the Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) interface. You can use the ViewLfETL.exe utility to view saved trace files. Server You can now create WMI scripts to list, create, delete, and attach repositories to your Laserfiche Server through the Windows Man

rows.title: Known Issues with the Laserfiche 7.0 Client.
rows.detailTitle: Known Issues with the Laserfiche 7.0 Client.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Known Issues with the Laserfiche 7.0 Client. Product: Laserfiche 7, Scanning 7 KB: 1000674 Published: July 1, 2005 Last Updated: July 1, 2005 Summary This article documents known issues with the Laserfiche 7.0 client. More Information Laserfiche Client Known Issues: 1000883 Renaming a Field Can Result in Data Loss. 1000697 Issues When Using the Laserfiche 6 Client with the Laserfiche 7 Server. 1000698 Loading the Laserfiche 6 Client and Laserfiche 7 Client on the Same Computer. 1000676 A "[7050] Open database failed: Cannot generate SSPI context." Error Occurs When Creating a New Repository. If you select a set of thumbnails and then choose to print, that set of thumbnails will not automatically be the specified page range for printing. (21520) The Laserfiche Repository Migration wizard will load the Attach Repository dialog box even if the migration process is unsuccessful. Click Cancel as there will be no migrated repository to attach. (14568) Adverse behavior can occur when detaching logical volumes. The detach process will not correctly export the physical subvolumes before removing them. This can result in data loss. (15221) If you have a Microsoft Office program installed, but have never started the program, you may encounter a configuration issue when you start the Laserfiche Client. You will be prompted for the Microsoft Office CD or given an error 1706. As a workaround, simply start any of the Microsoft Office programs. For more information, please see Microsoft knowledge base article: http://support.microsoft.com/default.aspx?scid=kb;en-us;298385. When sending e-mail from Outlook to Laserfiche, Laserfiche may not recognize that an e-mail contains an attachment. The Import Outlook Message dialog box may not list the e-mail's attachment(s). This prevents you from separating the attachment from the e-mail message. Import the e-mail message will always include attachments along with the message. (14299) The Folder Browser window cannot display the creation time for documents or folders created at 12:00:00 AM. The time portion of the creation date column value will be blank for such an entry. (14097) The Folder Browser window cannot display the time portion of a Time/Date field when the time entered is 12:00:00 AM. The time portion of the field column value will be blank for such an entry. (12973) Microsoft Outlook 2002 may display a "Virus attack" message each time Office Integration is used with Outlook. This is default Outlook behavior when adding a menu item to Outlook 2002. This is not a security issue involving a virus. (11762) Importing an .AFP file can cause Laserfiche to become non-responsive and timeout. This requires the user to manually end the task. (13813) An electronic document stored in a very long path cannot be opened using its native application. Laserfiche will make a failed attempt to open the electronic file with Snapshot. (11479) Color scanning does not work when using the Kodak scanner i840. Attempting to perform color scanning will generate the following message: cannot decompress image (61080). (13489) If you have two Laserfiche Servers attached in the Administration Console and you are using the .NET Remoting registry key, then they will both display the license information and repositories for the first Laserfiche Server. (14931) Importing a briefcase into a folder without sufficient rights generates the following error: "Invalid Briefcase Files(6396:0)" Laserfiche may become unstable. (852) Moving the processing dialog box when exporting a volume may cause Laserfiche to become non-responsive. (1778) Adding support for Arabic to Windows may prevent the creation of a new repository. Restart the SQL Server and Laserfiche Server services. (15351) Processing an electronic file with OCR will always process all pages, regardless of whether you have indicated that you would only like to process a select number of pages. (6272) On a Portuguese version of Windows, you may get an error message "Element not found" when trying to view the connected users in the Laserfiche Administration Console. (7324) The use of wildcard characters is not supported when searching integer, long integer, and date fields. (6909) If the folder path specified for an archiving audit events does not exist, you may receive a 7361 error. (16030) The Laserfiche Repository Migration wizard requires the Laserfiche 6 repository files to be located on the same computer as the Laserfiche Server (version 7). (16029) An administrator with the Manage Trustees privilege can delete Laserfiche user and group accounts. If this administrator user does not also have the Set Trustee Privileges privilege, deleting all user and group accounts can create a scenario where the remaining administrator is unable to recreate the full set of user accounts. (10881) When indexing large documents, the indexing progress bar may disappear before indexing is actually complete. Attempting to search prior to the c

rows.snippet: Release and Hotfix Information for Laserfiche Mobile 9.1 12 years ago support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013394/release-and-hotfix-information-for-laserfiche-mobile-9-1 Laserfiche App Summary Laserfiche Mobile Android app 9.1.0.303; Laserfiche... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013394/release-and-hotfix-informa ...
rows.title: Release and Hotfix Information for Laserfiche Mobile 9.1
rows.detailTitle: Release and Hotfix Information for Laserfiche Mobile 9.1
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release and Hotfix Information for Laserfiche Mobile 9.1 Product: Laserfiche App KB: 1013394 Published: December 30, 2013 Last Updated: May 12, 2014 Summary Laserfiche Mobile Android app 9.1.0.303; Laserfiche Mobile add-on 9.1.0.60 (Released December 31, 2013) This is a major release that includes a new Android app. 1013395 List of Changes for Laserfiche Mobile 9.1 1013393 Release Notes for Laserfiche Mobile 9.1 Updates for Laserfiche Mobile 9.1.0.324 (Released January 13, 2014) The Demo Account log in screen now displays the last email used to log into the app. (110865) Folders and documents now open more quickly. (111026) The device's back button now closes the app if the user is on the Welcome, Login, or Demo Login screen. (110870) The "Create a Demo Account" link is now easier to tap. (110865) The session timeout warning message now appears the first time the timeout is reached and appears correctly afterward. (110854) After you create a new library from the list of libraries that appears when you select to add an entry to a library, you will have the option to choose which library to add the entry to. (110784) Date and time fields can now be updated the first time you try after canceling the date and time pickers. (110804) If you try to log in to the Demo mode with an expired Demo Account, a run time error will no longer occur. (111103) A confirmation email is now set when you register a Demo Account with your support site credentials. (111094) If you select the "Remember Me" option, it will now remember the last successful login credentials after disconnecting from the server. (111095) Electronic documents will now upload with the correct extension format, even if the file includes a period (.) that is not followed by an extension. (110813) Fields in the lower part of the metadata view are now auto-scrolled to top when you tap in them. (110850) An error message no longer appears occasionally when you log out of the app. (110849) Mobile links are now generated with "lfmobiles" prefix when connecting to a Laserfiche Server with SSL enabled. (111097) An "Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation" error no longer occurs when returning to the Folder Browser after opening a document. (110869) When you rotate your device while viewing an electronic document in the app, the electronic document will now stay on the current page instead of jumping to the first page. (111025) An "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error no longer occurs when you click Register on the Demo Account registration screen. (111008) On the Demo Account registration page, you can now scroll to the bottom to see the "Terms and Condition" link. (111014) When downloading an electronic document, tapping outside the download prompt no longer cancels the download. (111040) The app no longer terminates unexpectedly when rotating the device while viewing electronic documents. (111053) After rotating a new document's image and then saving the document, the app now correctly goes to the Folder Browser. (111030) The app no longer crashes occasionally in Demo mode. (111024) If you fill in everything but the Email on the Demo Account registration page, you no longer get a "(405) Method not allowed" error. (1110011) The Demo Account registration page no longer shifts to the left. (111013) There is now a loading symbol when you log into a Demo Account or open the Document Viewer. (110967, 110968) PDFs now have a page number box that displays which page of the PDF you are viewing. (110871) After activating a Demo Account, you now have the option to go directly to the Google Play Store to download the app. (111031) Activating a Demo Account from the confirmation email now works correctly. (111032) On the Nexus 7, a loading symbol will no longer appear indefinitely when you open the metadata and no template or fields have been selected. (110754) Hotfix for the Laserfiche Mobile 9.1 Add-on (Released January 2, 2014) Install hotfix 1013397 to update the Laserfiche Mobile 9.1 Add-on for Web Access to the latest version. 1013397 Laserfiche Mobile 9.1 Add-on for Web Access Downloaded Between December 30th, 2013 and January 2nd, 2014 Cannot Be Configured from x86 Web Servers. Related Information See the following articles for information on previous versions of Laserfiche Mobile. 1013337 Release and Hotfix Information for Mobile 9.0.1 1013338 Release Notes for Laserfiche Mobile 9.0.1. 1013339 List of Changes for Laserfiche Mobile 9.0.1 1013212 Release Notes for Laserfiche Mobile 9.0 1013211 List of Changes for Laserfiche Mobile 9.0 Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: Release Notes for Workflow 10.2.1
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Workflow 10.2.1 Product: Workflow 10.2.1 KB: 1013965 Published: July 27, 2018 Last Updated: August 9, 2018 Summary Laserfiche Workflow 10.2.1 includes various feature improvements and bug fixes. For a detailed list of changes, please see Knowledge Base article 1013964. Laserfiche Workflow 10.2.1 is available as part of the Avante 10.2.1, Rio 10.2.1, Avante 10.3.1 and Rio 10.3.1 download packages after July 31, 2018. System Requirements Supported Operating Systems Windows 10 Windows 8.1 Windows Server 2012 R2 Windows Server 2012 Windows 7 (Service Pack 1) Windows Server 2008 R2 (Service Pack 1) Windows Server 2008 (Service Pack 2) Windows Server 2016 Note: 32-bit and 64-bit versions supported for all Workflow 10 and higher components. Note: If you are running Windows Server 2012, you must install the .NET Framework 4.5. Requirements for the Workflow Designer Minimum: CPU 1GHz, 512MB RAM. Recommended: CPU 2.4GHz, 1GB RAM or higher. Requirements for the Workflow Server and Subscriber Minimum: CPU 2.4GHz, 1GB RAM. Recommended: 2, 4, 6, or 8-core CPU, based on need. 4GB RAM or higher. More CPU cores allow more workflows to run simultaneously. Software Requirements Windows Components Windows Message Queuing component: This component must be manually installed on the machine(s) hosting the Workflow Server and Subscriber. Microsoft .NET Framework 4.5: This component will automatically be installed during the Workflow installation if it is not installed. Windows Components Necessary for the Workflow Web Service IIS (Internet Information Services): This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. Note: You must install IIS 7 or higher. IIS Basic Authentication and Windows Authentication: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. ASP.NET: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web Service. Note: If you are running Windows 8 or Windows 2012 Server, you must install the ASP.NET 4.5 feature in IIS. Windows Components for Workflow Web IIS 7 or higher (Internet Information Services): This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting Laserfiche Workflow Web. IIS Windows Authentication: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting the Workflow Web. ASP.NET 4.5: This component must be manually installed on the computer hosting Workflow Web. Web Browser: Internet Explorer 9 or later (Internet Explorer 10 or later recommended), Firefox 24, Chrome 30, Safari Supported Database Management Systems SQL Server 2008 SQL Server 2008 R2 SQL Server 2012 SQL Server 2014 SQL Server 2016 SQL Server 2017 Oracle 10g (10.2.0.4+) Oracle 11g (11.1.0.7+) Oracle 11g R2 (11.2.0.1+) and higher Oracle 12c Note: Express editions of the SQL Server versions listed above are also supprted but not recommended for high-volume Workflow installations. Note: If you are using an Oracle DBMS, the Oracle Client 11.2.0.2.1 with the corresponding version of Oracle Data Access Components (ODAC) must be installed on the machine hosting the Workflow Server. Note: If you are using Oracle 12c, please install the 11.2.0.3 Oracle Client on the machine hosting the Workflow Server. Laserfiche Components Laserfiche Server 8.0 or higher (Laserfiche Server 8.3.1 or higher is recommended.) Note: To use all aspects of the business process feature, you must have Laserfiche Server 9.0 or higher and the Laserfiche Client 9.0 and/or Web Access 9.0 or higher and/or Web Client. Component Known Issue Workflow Installation If you upgrade from Workflow 8.3.2 to Workflow 9.0.x and then immediately upgrade to Workflow 9.1 without first using the Workflow 9.0.x configuration manager to configure Workflow, the Workflow 9.1 upgrade may not retain your Workflow Server settings from Workflow 8.3.2. You can upgrade from Workflow 8.3.2 to Workflow 9.1. You do not need to do an intermediate upgrade to Workflow 9.0.x. (110343) Workflow Installation If you installed your Workflow Server and Subscriber on different computers, additional Microsoft Message Queuing configuration may be required. See the Workflow 9.1 Installation Guide for specific instructions. (78523) Workflow Installation If you installed your Workflow Server and Subscriber on different computers, you may need to provide domain authentication instead of using Local System Account when configuring the Workflow Subscriber. (77933) Workflow Installation When using the Repair option to restore assemblies that were deleted from the GAC, the process may fail. Repairing it a second time will succeed. (70389) Workflow Installation When upgrading to Workflow 9, if the Workflow 8.3 uninstall process fails, it must be manually uninstalled by running the original installer. (95866) Workflow Installation Uninstalling Workflow does not deactivate your license. Prior to uninstalling, you must delete the license in the Workflow Ad

rows.title: Known Issues with Laserfiche 7.2.1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Known Issues with Laserfiche 7.2.1. Product: Laserfiche 7.2.1 KB: 1011676 Published: November 1, 2006 Last Updated: February 3, 2016 Summary This article documents the list of known issues with Laserfiche 7.2.1. More Information See the following list of known issues for version 7.2.1. Product Known Issue Laserfiche Administration Console 1011212 Repository Registration When Using MSDE May Be Unexpectedly Slow. (26603) Laserfiche Administration Console 1011192 The Repository Registration Wizard, Repository Creation Wizard, and the Laserfiche 6 to 7 Migration Wizard Do Not Function On Computers Running Windows XP. (25904) Laserfiche Administration Console 1000966 The Laserfiche Administration Console Loads Slowly in Certain Situations. (15650) Laserfiche Administration Console When using the Laserfiche client, users that have not been granted a specific security tag are still able to see the existence of documents that have been associated with that security tag if they have been granted the Manage Entry Access privilege. However, this functionality is not fully available when using the Laserfiche Administration Console. This means that users with the Manage Entry Access privilege will not be able to use the Checked Out Documents item in the Laserfiche Administration Console to see checked out documents that are associated with a security tag that they do not have. (25025) Laserfiche Administration Console A Windows user account associated with a Laserfiche user account will not be copied when copying & pasting user accounts within the same repository when using the Laserfiche Administration Console. (18860) Client 1011905 An "Application failed to initialize properly (0xc0000005)." Error Occurs When Starting the Laserfiche Client Through a Citrix Session. Client 1011903 Laserfiche May Disconnect a User for Inactivity While the User is OCRing a Large Document. (33575) Client 1011894 Folder Browser Columns Display Date Fields as a String Value Instead of in Date Format. Client 1011893 Laserfiche Cannot Import Certain Invalid Image Files. (32578) Client 1011891 An "An unrecognized token '')'' was found." Error Occurs When Opening a PDF File Exported from Laserfiche. (33401) Client 1011890 You are Unable to E-mail Documents From Laserfiche Using the GroupWise E-mail Client. Client 1011885 The View Image and View Text Options are Unavailable in the Document Viewer. Client 1011880 You Cannot Drag and Drop an Electronic Document From Laserfiche Into the Body of your E-mail Message. (29818) Client 1011864 A "Failed to initialize metadata display. Unknown error. [340]" Error Occurs When Opening a Document or Viewing Metadata. Client 1011862 You Cannot Drag and Drop Items Into your Repository. Client 1011858 Laserfiche May Not Properly Export Images in TIFF JPEG format. Client 1011852 An "I/O Error in Display Engine [6603]" Error Occurs When Exporting Documents and Folders. (31322) Client 1011843 OmniPage OCR Does Not Support the "Optimize for speed" Option when OCRing Asian Languages. (29273) Client 1011842 OmniPage OCR Does Not Correctly Process Color Images When OCRing Asian Languages. (29281) Client 1011841 OmniPage OCR Does Not Correctly Process Multi-Byte Western Languages.(29919) Client 1011840 Temporary OCR Files Are Not Automatically Removed When OCRing Color or Grayscale Images. (29378) Client 1011839 OmniPage OCR Cannot Process E-sized Images. (27647) Client 1011831 Versions of Adobe Acrobat and Reader Prior to 7.0.5 Do Not Include IFilter Functionality. Client 1011825 Laserfiche Does Not Generate Text When you Choose to Extract Text from Certain PDF files. Client 1011823 The .reg Files included with the Legacy OCR Adapter Installation Package are not Compatible with 64-bit Editions of Windows. Client 1011821 A "Failed to determine process for document ''?'' [6000]" Error Occurs When OCRing Shortcuts. (29975) Client 1011817 An "Internal Error 2739." Error Occurs When Installing Laserfiche. Client 1011794 The "Send Message" Option in the Laserfiche Administration Console is Not Supported on Windows Vista. Client 1011730 An "Error 1904" Error Occurs When Installing the Laserfiche Client. Client 1011746 Office Integration and Text Extraction May Not Function Correctly With Microsoft Office 2007. (28596) Client 1011744 Laserfiche Silently Fails when you Drag-and-Drop E-mail Messages into Laserfiche if the E-mail has a Subject Containing Special Characters. (28390) Client 1011727 An "MMC.exe has encountered a problem and needs to close." Error Occurs When Loading the Laserfiche Administration Console. Client 1011742 You Cannot Drag and Drop Attachments Within an E-mail Message From Outlook Express 6 to Laserfiche. (28928) Client 1011741 Laserfiche May Disconnect a User for Inactivity Prior to the Specified Inactivity Time Limit. (28473) Client 1011725 OmniPage OCR Does Not Correctly Use the Image Orientation Displayed in the Laserfiche Client When OCRing. (28993) Client 1011715

Error Occurs When Creating a New Repository. The option to create custom toolbar buttons i... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000929/known-issues-with-laserfiche-clie ...
rows.title: Known Issues with Laserfiche Client 7.0.3.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Known Issues with Laserfiche Client 7.0.3. Product: Laserfiche 7.0.3 KB: 1000929 Published: April 4, 2005 Last Updated: April 4, 2005 Summary This article documents known issues with Laserfiche Client 7.0.3. More Information Laserfiche Client Known Issues 1000807 A "The repository was not found [7023]" Error Occurs When Unregistering a Local Repository. (17330) 1000697 Issues When Using the Laserfiche 6 Client with the Laserfiche 7 Server. 1000698 Using the Laserfiche 6 Client and Laserfiche 7 Client on the Same Computer. 1000676 A "[7050] Open database failed: Cannot generate SSPI context." Error Occurs When Creating a New Repository. The option to create custom toolbar buttons is only available for the Folder Browser window. You cannot create custom toolbar buttons for the Document window. (20117) Pressing the Q key in the Folders pane may not take you to the first item with a name that starts with Q. Instead, it may allow you to edit the name of the currently selected item in the Folders pane. (19798) Users denied the Read field access right on all fields in a template might still be able to see the name of the template. Field data remains secure where unauthorized users will not be able to see the field names or field data. However, they may be able to see the name of the template. (19620) If you use the Send to Laserfiche option from within Microsoft Outlook to send an e-mail with a multi-page TIFF attachment, only the first page is created. As a workaround, drag and drop the e-mail with the attachment into Laserfiche. With this action, all the pages of the multi-page TIFF will be successfully created. (19568) When using Laserfiche Scanning, scanned documents that are then processed by OCR may not be automatically added to the indexing queue if the current user inherits the Process feature right from a Laserfiche group. (19301) The Outlook New Message window appears when sending a Laserfiche document as an e-mail attachment. If the main Microsoft Outlook application is also open, you will be unable to toggle between the two Outlook windows. Only the Outlook New Message window will be available while it is open. (18482) The Laserfiche Administration Console only allows one audit report to be opened at a single time. (17733) The Send to Laserfiche feature does not allow you to send documents to Laserfiche without a specified template. You will receive an error 6000 if you attempt to send a document without specifying a template. (17680) An Invalid pointer (-2147467261) error can occur from the Laserfiche Administration Console if you right-click on Index and select Properties without first clicking on the Index node. (17669) Laserfiche Scanning does not take into account that the while the record series code is displayed in the folder tree, it is currently not part of the actual path. (16850) When upgrading the Laserfiche client to version 7.0.3, the installation expects the installation package to be located in the same path where the original installation package was located. This means that you should install from the same CD-ROM drive you used for the original installation. (16825) Migrating documents to another volume from the results of a full-text search will return an "Object locked [7390]" error. (16839) If you have a Microsoft Office program installed, but have never started the program, you may encounter a configuration issue when you start the Laserfiche Client. You will be prompted for the Microsoft Office CD or given an error 1706. As a workaround, simply start any Microsoft Office program. For more information, please see Microsoft knowledge base article: http://support.microsoft.com/default.aspx?scid=kb;en-us;298385. When sending e-mail from Outlook to Laserfiche, Laserfiche may not recognize that an e-mail contains an attachment. As a result, the Import Outlook Message dialog box may not list the e-mail's attachment(s). This prevents you from separating the attachment from the e-mail message. Import the e-mail message will always include attachments along with the message. (14299) The Folder Browser window cannot display the creation/modification time for documents or folders created at 12:00:00 AM. The time portion of the creation date column value will be blank for such an entry. (14097) The Folder Browser window cannot display the time portion of a Time/Date field when the time entered is 12:00:00 AM. The time portion of the field column value will be blank for such an entry. (12973) Microsoft Outlook 2002 may display a "Virus attack" message each time Office Integration is used with Outlook. This is default Outlook behavior when adding a menu item to Outlook 2002. This is not a security issue involving a virus. (11762) An electronic document stored in a very long path cannot be opened using its native application. Laserfiche will make a failed attempt to open the electronic file with Snapshot. (11479) Color scanning does not work when using the Kodak
